destinations.evidence: The RPC handler synchronizes verifier context, requires a successful SPC verification result, and only then submits the async result to the pool as fulfilled.
Block verification reconstructs expected SPC data from AsyncJobTracker state and rejects the block if signature verification errors or returns false.

equivalence_argument: RPC fulfillment and block validation use the same SPC signature primitive and both fail closed before state transition or block acceptance when executor proof verification fails.
notes: Mapped through port RPC submitAsyncResult and block-verification SPC checks.
rationale: The FSM documentation marks immediate verification as critical and separately describes post-execution SPC verification as a condition for block acceptance. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async executor results must be signature-verified against the registered executor public key before the RPC handler moves a transaction to fulfilled, and validators must reject a proposed block if any attached SPC proof fails verification.
suggested_falsification_tests: Submit an async result signed by an unregistered or wrong executor key and assert ritual_submitAsyncResult rejects it without moving the transaction to fulfilled; separately propose a block containing an SPC call with an invalid proof and assert validation rejects the block.
summary: Executor proofs gate both RPC fulfillment and validator block acceptance.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Submit an async result signed by an unregistered/wrong executor and assert RPC rejection; construct a settlement block with invalid SPC proof and assert block verification rejects it.
title: Executor proofs gate fulfillment and block acceptance
